paper_title: Materials and Methods for Producing Seedless Watermelon Using Soft-X-Irradiated Pollen

methods.description: This method involves irradiating male watermelon flowers with soft-X-rays to produce pollen that can be used to create seedless watermelon fruits. The irradiation process is used to reduce the germination rate of the pollen, which affects fertilization and embryo formation.

steps.description: The male flowers were picked on the morning of anthesis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect male watermelon flowers at anthesis

steps.description: The blooming male flowers were irradiated with a 800 Gy dose (11.1 Gy/min) of soft-X-ray.
steps.searchable_summary: irradiate male flowers with 800 Gy soft-X-ray

steps.description: Irradiated pollen was immediately used for pollination.

methods.method_name: Observation of Fertilization and Embryo Formation
methods.description: This method involves selecting and observing watermelon fruits at various time intervals after pollination with either irradiated or unirradiated pollen to study fertilization and embryo formation.

steps.description: A minimum of five fruits was selected at random at various time intervals after pollination for observation.
steps.searchable_summary: select fruits at specified intervals for observation

steps.description: Five watermelon ovaries were cut equatorially and rapidly fixed with FAA solution.
steps.searchable_summary: cut and fix watermelon ovaries with FAA solution
